title: Jesus, Thou Joy of loving hearts
firstLine: Jesus, Thou Joy of loving hearts,
textAttribution: Text: Bernard of Clairvaux (1090–1153), Ray Palmer (1808–1887).
publicDomainBasis: Bernard of Clairvaux (1090–1153), Ray Palmer (1808–1887), Henry Percy Smith (1825–1898). All public domain.

sections.label: Verse 1
sections.text:
Jesus, Thou Joy of loving hearts,
Thou Fount of life, Thou Light of men,
From the best bliss that earth imparts,
We turn unfilled to Thee again.

sections.label: Verse 2
Verse 3
sections.text:
We taste Thee, O Thou living Bread,
And long to feast upon Thee still;
We drink of Thee, the Fountain-head,
And thirst our souls from Thee to fill!

sections.label: Verse 4
sections.text:
Our restless spirits yearn for Thee,
Where’er our changeful lot is cast;
Glad when Thy gracious smile we see,
Blest, when our faith can hold Thee fast.

sections.label: Verse 5
sections.text:
O Lord, be Thou our strength and stay!
Make all our moments calm and bright,
Chase all dark thoughts of sin away,
Shed o’er us here Thy holy light.
